title: Restaurant Menu Scraping
html:
The scrappy data-acquisition method Yahir used to bootstrap the fast food nutrition dataset for BroMenu / Brotein: open the chain's corporate nutrition PDF (or the website menu detail panels), screenshot the relevant tables, OCR them, and parse the result into the Supabase menu_items table. Apple Vision OCR was the entry point — fast and free but unreliable on tabular layouts.

The scrape sprint is concentrated in two windows. July 9 2025 (2025 07 09 screenshot 2025 07 09 at 4 51 29 pm 720cb8bf) shows the Supabase schema was already in place. Eight days later, between July 17 evening and July 18 5 a.m., Yahir grabbed a tight cluster of nutrition PDFs: Chipotle (2025 07 17 screenshot 2025 07 17 at 9 13 52 pm bb1a5662, the proof case because Chipotle is user-assembled), then Little Caesars at 2:41 a.m., Wingstop at 3:10 a.m., and Taco Bell in a 17-second triple-shot at 4:56:13 / 4:56:23 / 4:56:30 a.m. The timestamp pattern (scrolled and snapped, scrolled and snapped) confirms intent — this was a data sprint, not a curiosity tour.

The recurring failure mode is OCR column drift on tabular nutrition PDFs. The Chipotle summary explicitly flags it: cheese (1 oz) reading as 480 cal when the canonical value is ~110, guacamole 4 oz reading as 540 when real is ~230, sour cream 15 cal when real is ~110. Each chain summary independently calls out the same problem, leading to a converging recommendation: long-term, swap Apple Vision OCR for direct PDF text-extraction or hit the chains' undocumented JSON menu endpoints. The Taco Bell capture also revealed that the underlying source was the website (the (more info) anchors), not the PDF — so the right scrape strategy is chain-specific.

Two adjacent challenges keep recurring: variant explosion (Taco Bell has 30+ chiller/refresca SKUs across sugar/zero-sugar × size; Wingstop has 13 wing flavors with identical macros except sodium) and bowl-vs-item semantics (Chipotle is assembled; everyone else is purchased). Both push schema design toward canonicalization-at-insert and synonym-mapped fuzzy matching.

Source summaries

  • 2025 07 09 screenshot 2025 07 09 at 4 51 29 pm 720cb8bf — schema target
  • 2025 07 17 screenshot 2025 07 17 at 9 13 52 pm bb1a5662 — Chipotle (proof case + OCR-drift confession)
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 2 41 11 am f76d9c99 — Little Caesars
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 3 10 37 am fedfeec5 — Wingstop
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 13 am e3d1a9e8 — Taco Bell pt 1
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 23 am 02657aa9 — Taco Bell pt 2

Connections

  • fast food nutrition dataset — what's being built
  • supabase — destination database
  • bromenu / brotein / anabolic scoring — downstream uses
  • openai api — paired AI for OCR/scoring
  • bromenu data model — schema for canonicalization
